title: BLUE VerifierPlus Frequently Asked Questions
whyTrust1: BLUE VerifierPlus is based on open source software developed by the <dccLink>Digital Credentials Consortium</dccLink>, a network of leading international universities designing an open infrastructure for digital academic credentials.
whyTrust2: This instance has been evolved for <blueLink>BLUE</blueLink>, the academic trust network operated by <redirisLink>RedIRIS</redirisLink> (Red.es) in collaboration with <crueLink>CRUE Universidades Españolas</crueLink>.
whyTrust3: This website implements <githubLink>open source libraries</githubLink> that support open technical standards for supported digital credentials.
formatsTitle: What formats of digital academic credentials are supported?
formatsIntro: BLUE VerifierPlus supports digital academic credentials:

formatsList.w3c: Using the W3C Verifiable Credential Data Model v1.1 and v2.0

formatsRequirements: In addition, credentials must support the following standards and specifications for full verification:

requirementsList.registry: The issuer must exist in a supported registry (EBSI, BLUE, or DCC Known Issuers).
requirementsList.did: The issuer and subject decentralized identifiers must be did:key, did:web, did:jwk, did:ebsi, or did:blue
requirementsList.crypto: The appropriate cryptographic signing method must be used

publicLink: Users of the BLUE Learner Credential Wallet mobile app are able to create Public Links if they wish to share a credential from their wallet to anyone with the link.
contactTitle: Who do I contact if I have more questions?
contact1: For questions about BLUE VerifierPlus please email <emailLink>blue@rediris.es</emailLink>.
contact2: To learn more about BLUE please visit <blueWikiLink>wiki.rediris.es/spaces/BLUE</blueWikiLink>.
